disclosure_items.clinical_fact: 72-hour flare of inflammatory facial and truncal spots with about 6 hours of continuous excoriation causing raw, bleeding and weeping surfaces
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and how has it changed?
How long has he been scratching, and when did the bleeding begin?
Can you talk me through how the last few days have gone?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The spots got worse over about three days, and tonight he's been scratching them non-stop for hours — that's when they started bleeding and weeping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe itch is driving a compulsive itch-scratch cycle
trigger_question_examples: Is the itch the main thing bothering him?
What is making him scratch so much?
How bad is the itching for him?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it's the itch — he says it itches so much he can't stop, and the more he scratches the worse it gets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is afebrile and systemically well — no fever, no feeling generally unwell
trigger_question_examples: Does he have a temperature or feel hot or shivery?
Is he otherwise well in himself — eating, drinking, no fever?
Any signs he's generally unwell, not just the skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he doesn't feel hot and he hasn't got a temperature that I can tell — he's eating and drinking fine, he's just upset about the itching.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really checked anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spreading redness, warmth or swelling in the skin surrounding the excoriated lesions
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin around the sore spots spreading red, hot or swollen?
Do the areas around the spots look like they're getting redder or puffier?
Is there any red streak spreading out from the spots?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the skin around them doesn't look like it's spreading or gone hot and swollen — it's just the scratched spots themselves that are raw.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: They just look sore where he's scratched, I think.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute behavioural agitation with compulsive skin-picking; support worker is present and currently able to supervise, and the environment has been made safe (no sharps accessible)
trigger_question_examples: How is he coping behaviourally — is he safe and are you able to manage him?
Is he at risk of hurting himself, and can you keep him safe right now?
How agitated is he, and do you have support there?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's agitated and pacing and keeps picking, but I can be with him and keep an eye on him. We've got the place set up safely and there's nothing sharp he can get to.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has an intellectual disability and requires carer-mediated history and care, with limited capacity to self-manage skin care or recognise deterioration
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me a bit about him and how he manages day to day?
Who looks after his care and can he manage things himself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has an intellectual disability so he needs a fair bit of support — he can't really do skin care himself, so I'd be the one doing anything you suggest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No current topical or systemic skin treatments; no active acne prescriptions on record
trigger_question_examples: Is he on any creams, gels or tablets for his skin?
Has he had any treatment for these spots before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he's not on anything for his skin at the moment — no creams or tablets for it.
